title: The Cabin Show

jokes.text: Visit, no. Gloat, yes.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he's going to see his imprisoned father
jokes.punchline: The honest admission of petty motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Michael's self-awareness about his pettiness. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: You seem more villainous than usual, Mom. Are you sober?
jokes.setup: Michael notices his mother acting strangely
jokes.punchline: He assumes sobriety is the cause of her being worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dysfunction joke - sobriety making someone worse, not better. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: And that's how long I've been depressed about him.
jokes.setup: Michael points out she's been on postpartum medication for 32 years
jokes.punchline: She's been depressed about having Buster for his entire life
jokes.explanation: Devastatingly dark maternal rejection delivered matter-of-factly. Peak Lucille character work.

jokes.text: Claiming she could take it no more, the young mother released the emergency brake, allowing her car to roll backwards into the nearby lake.
jokes.setup: Lucille mentions not liking what happened when she went off medication in the early '90s
jokes.punchline: Dramatic suicide attempt via emergency brake release
jokes.explanation: Absurdly overwrought flashback to mundane medication side effects. Visual comedy through contrast.

jokes.text: Narrator describes Lucille's 90s suicide attempt, followed by her dismissive 'Good for her'
jokes.setup: Dramatic description of young mother's suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Lucille casually approves of her past self's actions
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - tragic suicide attempt treated as reasonable response to Buster. The casual approval is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Good for her.
jokes.setup: Narrator describes her dramatic suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Lucille approves of her own past suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to her own melodrama. Maximum economy with dark twist.

jokes.text: I must have gained three pounds.
jokes.setup: After describing her suicide attempt as the consequence of going off medication
jokes.punchline: The real problem was minor weight gain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - the 'consequences' were actually vanity concerns, not mental health. Peak Lucille.

jokes.text: That was Tom Cruise, the actor.
jokes.setup: Lucille mentions a handsome young doctor on The Today Show
jokes.punchline: It was Tom Cruise during his anti-psychiatry phase
jokes.explanation: Timely reference to Tom Cruise's Scientology anti-drug stance, but somewhat dated now.

jokes.text: How am I supposed to find someone willing to go into that musty old claptrap?
jokes.setup: Michael asking about finding someone for the cabin
jokes.punchline: Could refer to either the cabin or a woman
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre setup for Lucille's response. Good structure and wordplay.

jokes.text: Hey, pal, something's come up. This nice young lady has lost her puppy, and your dad's got to help her find him.
jokes.setup: Young Gob waiting to go to the cabin with his father
jokes.punchline: Transparent excuse involving a lost puppy
jokes.explanation: Classic bad father excuse, establishes pattern but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Mine's sadder.
jokes.setup: Michael and Gob comparing childhood disappointments about the cabin
jokes.punchline: Gob competitively claims his story is sadder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob character moment - making trauma into competition. Great economy.

jokes.text: Gob and Michael compete over who had the sadder childhood regarding the cabin
jokes.setup: Both brothers were disappointed about the cabin
jokes.punchline: They literally compete over whose disappointment was worse
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry moment - turning childhood trauma into competition. Very true to their characters.

jokes.text: You taste these tears. Taste my sadness.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to prove he's sadder than Michael
jokes.punchline: Literally offering his tears for Michael to taste
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - literalizing emotion sharing. Peak Gob weirdness.

jokes.text: But the one thing that I will never do is not tell him that I'm taking him to a cabin in the woods and then not take him.
jokes.setup: Gob's convoluted promise about how he'll treat his hypothetical son
jokes.punchline: The triple negative creates confusing logic
jokes.explanation: Deliberately confusing wordplay that reveals Gob's twisted logic. Low economy but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Gob's convoluted logic about what he'll do with his hypothetical son regarding cabin trips
jokes.setup: Gob declares what he would do differently as a father
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily complex logic about cabin promises vs. actual cabin trips
jokes.explanation: Very Gob - overthinking simple concepts into incomprehensible rules. Foreshadows his actual parenting situation.

jokes.text: Taste the happy, Michael. Taste it.
jokes.setup: Gob excited about going to the cabin
jokes.punchline: Reverses the earlier 'taste my sadness' bit
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Gob's consistency in weird behavior patterns.

jokes.text: Michael had never been so touched by something his son had said, but it wasn't true.
jokes.setup: George Michael claiming he slept outside to see his father
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately undercuts the touching moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant narrative misdirection that immediately deflates sentiment. Classic AD structure.

jokes.text: He was hiding from his cousin after an awkward incident.
jokes.setup: Revealing why George Michael was really outside
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable truth about cousin attraction
jokes.explanation: Sets up major running plot thread while explaining the misdirection. Good character work.

jokes.text: - Hi. - Hi. - I'm off to the car. - I'll be in the crawl space.
jokes.setup: The cousins awkwardly encountering each other
jokes.punchline: They immediately flee in opposite directions to uncomfortable places
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkwardness captured in minimal dialogue. Great economy showing their avoidance.

jokes.text: I think the President of a 'Don't Buy' company can afford to take a day off.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking if Michael can skip work
jokes.punchline: References the earlier stock rating joke
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Michael's ability to laugh at their situation.

jokes.text: There's only one sleeping bag, so we're gonna have to double up. Pigs in a blanket.
jokes.setup: Father-son camping preparation
jokes.punchline: Innocent food metaphor becomes awkward given the circumstances
jokes.explanation: Food metaphor creates slight discomfort in father-son context. Mild wordplay.

jokes.text: It is worse to know.
jokes.setup: Michael's fear of becoming his father
jokes.punchline: George Michael's wise but devastating observation
jokes.explanation: Profound wisdom delivered with perfect economy. Captures the generational trauma theme.

jokes.text: Unfortunately for Oscar, 'You've got the wrong twin' was a very popular alibi.
jokes.setup: Oscar claiming mistaken identity
jokes.punchline: Apparently this is a common criminal excuse
jokes.explanation: Funny undermining of Oscar's legitimate claim by making it seem like a cliché.

jokes.text: We're quadruplets. You got the wrong two.
jokes.setup: Examples of the 'wrong twin' alibi
jokes.punchline: Escalates to quadruplets with mathematical precision
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the alibi concept. The precision of 'wrong two' is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you're Oscar. Dot com.
jokes.setup: Michael finally believing Oscar's story
jokes.punchline: Adds 'dot com' as if it's part of his name
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that treats the domain as part of his identity. Good economy.

jokes.text: We're super rich again. And I'm gonna buy a car, the Volvo.
jokes.setup: Lindsay excited about the company's stock improvement
jokes.punchline: She's sitting on what looks like a Volvo logo from copying
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag playing with copying machine patterns and brand recognition.

jokes.text: That's from sitting on the copier.
jokes.setup: Lindsay pointing to what she thinks is a Volvo
jokes.punchline: Michael matter-of-factly explains the copier pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man delivery explaining the visual gag. Michael's matter-of-fact tone sells it.

jokes.text: All right, maybe you're right. Maybe we should get you this car. Yeah, I don't know. It's so boxy.
jokes.setup: Michael testing Lindsay's want vs. have theory
jokes.punchline: Lindsay immediately loses interest when offered what she wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Michael's psychology theory. Lindsay's instant reversal is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Yeah, like I'd ever hear from that son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if George Sr. contacted Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob denies it right before we learn he did
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate contradiction. Gob's bitterness contrasts with his secret hope.

jokes.text: Earlier that day, Gob received a letter from a group that reunited abandoned sons with their fathers, called S.A.D.
jokes.setup: Narrator revealing Gob did hear from his father
jokes.punchline: The organization acronym perfectly describes the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect acronym that captures both the service and emotional state. Smart wordplay.

jokes.text: Barry says a lot of things he doesn't mean.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Barry's location
jokes.punchline: Secretary's matter-of-fact assessment of Barry's dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Barry's incompetence. Secretary's resigned tone is key.

jokes.text: Kind of feel like that kid who found the severed hand.
jokes.setup: Steve Holt being stood up by his father
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to a traumatic discovery
jokes.explanation: Bizarrely dark but character-appropriate comparison. Shows Steve's warped perspective on disappointment.

jokes.text: If Steve had had a father, he would've warned him not to go into the woods with strange men. But he didn't.
jokes.setup: Gob inviting Steve to the cabin
jokes.punchline: Lack of father figure creates dangerous naivety
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark irony about parental protection. The circular logic is perfect.

jokes.text: Michael, meanwhile, arrived in Reno, a city competing with Las Vegas' successful 'What happens in Vegas stays in Vegas' campaign
jokes.setup: Michael arriving in Reno
jokes.punchline: Reno's inferior marketing compared to Vegas
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about city marketing. Not particularly character-specific.

jokes.text: The Christian League had the Seedy Underbelly shut down. Now it's a Swallows.
jokes.setup: Michael looking for seedy underbelly of Reno
jokes.punchline: Replacement name 'Swallows' is equally suggestive
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre joke about moral crusades missing the point. Solid wordplay.

jokes.text: Swallows was a family-style restaurant by day, and an anything-goes pan-sexual bazaar by night.
jokes.setup: Description of Swallows restaurant
jokes.punchline: Extreme contrast between day and night activities
jokes.explanation: Good contrast joke about dual-purpose establishments. The formal tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That is until he choked on a marshmallow and almost died when no one noticed he turned blue.
jokes.setup: George Sr. hiding as Blue Man
jokes.punchline: Can't show distress because he's supposed to be blue
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - the disguise prevents rescue. Perfect situational comedy with dark edge.

jokes.text: Leaving Lindsay was the biggest little mistake I've ever made.
jokes.setup: Tobias reflecting on his marriage
jokes.punchline: Contradictory description of the mistake's size
jokes.explanation: Typical Tobias wordplay that reveals his confusion about the situation's importance.

jokes.text: Say goodbye to these!
jokes.setup: Kitty breaking up with Tobias
jokes.punchline: Her signature move of flashing, but they're blue from paint
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character trait with visual twist of blue paint.

jokes.text: Those are blue too.
jokes.setup: Kitty's dramatic flashing exit
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact observation about the blue paint
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic moment with practical observation. Great economy.

jokes.text: What's a leather pony?
jokes.setup: Tobias mentioning setting up the leather pony
jokes.punchline: His innocent question about sexual equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias naivety about sexual topics. His innocent delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I don't know. We don't have that at lunch.
jokes.setup: Tobias asking about leather pony
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact response about restaurant timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response treating sexual equipment like a menu item.

jokes.text: And the kids found themselves closer than ever on the bumpy road to Reno.
jokes.setup: George Michael and Maeby in car together
jokes.punchline: Physical bumping forces unwanted intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good setup for their awkward situation. The narrator's knowing tone adds to the discomfort.

jokes.text: What if Kitty's not there and I'm less of a Blue Man and more of a cocktail waitress?
jokes.setup: Tobias planning to meet Lindsay at Blue Man show
jokes.punchline: Suggests he might be working as cocktail waitress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias gender confusion delivered as casual aside. Character-perfect obliviousness.

jokes.text: You're my only client. What am I supposed to do now? Turn tricks on the street?
jokes.setup: Michael firing Barry as lawyer
jokes.punchline: Barry considers prostitution as career alternative
jokes.explanation: Typical Barry inappropriate response. His casual consideration of prostitution is character-perfect.

jokes.text: How much do you think I can get for that, by way? $50, or is that just L.A.?
jokes.setup: Barry considering prostitution
jokes.punchline: He's actually pricing it out geographically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Barry treating prostitution like market research. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: Let's get it out of the way.
jokes.setup: Cousins discussing their awkward kiss situation
jokes.punchline: They decide to finish the kiss like a chore
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkwardness - treating romantic gesture like an obligation. Great character development.

jokes.text: Isn't it behind you?
jokes.setup: George Michael claiming to watch Reno sunset
jokes.punchline: Maeby points out he's facing the wrong direction
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about his obvious excuse-making. Maeby's practical observation deflates his drama.

jokes.text: You're the dad.
jokes.setup: Gob not realizing his relationship to Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: Michael's simple statement of obvious truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in delivering major realization. Michael's matter-of-fact tone is key.

jokes.text: I've made a huge tiny mistake.
jokes.setup: Gob realizing he's about to abandon his son
jokes.punchline: Contradictory description of mistake size
jokes.explanation: Nice variation on 'huge mistake' catchphrase. The contradiction shows Gob's confusion.

jokes.text: No, I'm better.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing himself to his father
jokes.punchline: Simple assertion of moral superiority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael self-awareness and determination. Maximum economy in character development.

jokes.text: It's never happening.
jokes.setup: George Michael saying camping is 'finally happening'
jokes.punchline: Maeby's pessimistic but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby cynicism delivered with maximum economy. Contrasts with George Michael's hope.

jokes.text: Although, I was very close. I almost had Pop-pop in Reno.
jokes.setup: Michael prioritizing family time over finding father
jokes.punchline: Uses George Michael's pet name for his grandfather
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Michael adopting his son's perspective and language.

jokes.text: Me, too.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he almost had grandfather in Reno
jokes.punchline: George Michael's innocent agreement with different meaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect double meaning - George Michael referring to different kind of 'having' with cousin. Great economy.

jokes.text: Well, that was a freebie.
jokes.setup: Lucille arriving at cabin to find Buster's hook accidentally caused damage
jokes.punchline: She treats accidental destruction as a bonus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille darkness - finding silver lining in destruction. Character-perfect callousness.

jokes.text: If this tableau I recreate, perhaps I can re-snare my mate.
jokes.setup: Tobias planning to fake being with Kitty
jokes.punchline: Overly flowery rhyming speech
jokes.explanation: Typical Tobias pretentious language. The forced rhyme scheme is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I decided to sleep in the car so my snoring wouldn't bother you, and I left that recording of my snoring so you wouldn't know I'm gone.
jokes.setup: Buster explaining his consideration for others
jokes.punchline: Convoluted logic using recording of the problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback with twisted Buster logic. The solution creates the original problem.

jokes.text: This is just like when we used to live up in the attic.
jokes.setup: Buster enjoying cabin time with mother
jokes.punchline: References inappropriate living situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster oversharing about inappropriate mother-son closeness. Dark family dysfunction.

jokes.text: Well, every problem has an answer. You're probably just confused because you're going through all of this alone.
jokes.setup: George Michael's cousin attraction problem
jokes.punchline: Michael's innocent advice about not going through things alone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - Michael's well-meaning advice is exactly wrong for this situation.

jokes.text: That and cheating on Gangy.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining what the cabin is for
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of grandfather's infidelity
jokes.explanation: Perfect casual delivery of dark family history. Explains the cabin's real purpose.

jokes.text: There's no cabin.
jokes.setup: Characters arriving at cabin location
jokes.punchline: The cabin is literally gone
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of episode's central goal. Maximum economy in delivering the disappointment.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Are you still doing breakfast burritos?
jokes.setup: Maeby finding someone at the hotel to help forget her cousin
jokes.punchline: She recognizes him as a breakfast burrito vendor
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - what seemed like romantic encounter is actually food service recognition.

jokes.text: I want a divorce.
jokes.setup: Lindsay catching Tobias with fake Kitty
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to divorce
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay dramatic overreaction. Perfect character response to perceived betrayal.

jokes.text: And I would be thrilled to represent either one of you. That'll be $50.
jokes.setup: Lindsay declaring divorce
jokes.punchline: Barry immediately offering legal services with pricing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry opportunism with callback to his pricing discussions. Character-consistent greed.

tmdb_overview: Michael learns that it's actually George Sr.'s twin brother Oscar serving time in prison, not George. After getting a tip from Barry, Michael heads to Reno to find George, but that costs him a camping trip he promised his son.
insight: Season 3 opener sustains 2.4 jokes per minute across 64 gags without a single standout moment.
